one_line: Emperor of the Pala dynasty ruling Bengal and Bihar as the Ganga’s guardian king.

appearance.age_look: Early 40s, tall and broad-shouldered with a sturdy warrior-king build.
appearance.face: Oval face with a strong jaw, medium-brown complexion, straight nose, neatly trimmed full moustache and short beard framing his mouth and chin; sharp, intense eyes with thick brows, default expression calm but commanding with a hint of sternness.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair gathered into a high topknot at the back of the crown, sides smoothly pulled back; a narrow gold circlet with a small central red gemstone sits low on the forehead, holding the hair in place.
appearance.costume: A knee-length, long-sleeved reddish-maroon silk tunic (inspired by early medieval eastern Indian royal attire) with a subtle woven floral motif and a round neckline edged in gold; over it a sleeveless, slightly shorter golden-yellow silk over-tunic with broad borders in deep green and maroon, decorated with small round motifs; a wide, ornamented gold cloth belt (kati-bandha) cinching the waist, with a hanging central tasselled knot; lower garment is an off-white cotton dhoti wrapped in the eastern style, pleated in front and reaching mid-calf, with a maroon and green border; over one shoulder a deep forest-green silk uttariya (shawl) draped diagonally across the chest and flowing behind; jewellery includes a pair of medium-length gold kundala earrings, a single broad gold necklace with a central pendant, a simpler second chain, two thick gold bangles on each wrist, and an upper armlet on the right arm shaped like a coiled serpent; feet in simple brown leather sandals; no armor plates but a subtle quilted padding visible under the tunic around the shoulders and chest to suggest battle readiness.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly curved steel sword with a simple straight crossguard and disc pommel, sheathed in a dark brown leather scabbard with gold fittings, worn at his left side on a fabric baldric; the hilt is wrapped in dark red leather and the pommel bears a small engraved lotus motif.

ref_desc: Mahipala is a tall, broad-shouldered, early-40s Pala king with medium-brown skin, black hair in a high topknot held by a gold circlet, maroon silk tunic, gold-yellow over-tunic, off-white dhoti, green shawl, gold jewellery, sandals, and a curved sword in a brown scabbard with red-wrapped hilt.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Mahipala, a tall, broad-shouldered early-40s Pala emperor from 11th-century Bengal, with medium-brown skin, an oval face, strong jaw, neat moustache and short beard, intense eyes and thick brows, and thick black hair pulled into a high topknot held by a narrow gold circlet with a small red gem; he wears a knee-length long-sleeved reddish-maroon silk tunic with subtle floral motifs and a gold-edged round neckline, over it a sleeveless slightly shorter golden-yellow silk over-tunic with deep green and maroon borders and small round motifs, an ornate gold cloth belt tied at the waist with a central tassel, an off-white cotton dhoti with maroon and green border wrapped to mid-calf, a deep forest-green silk uttariya draped from his left shoulder across the chest and flowing behind, subtle quilted padding showing under the tunic at shoulders and chest, gold kundala earrings, two layered gold necklaces, gold bangles on both wrists and a serpent-shaped armlet on his right upper arm, and simple brown leather sandals; at his left side hangs his signature slightly curved steel sword in a dark brown leather scabbard with gold fittings, the hilt wrapped in dark red leather with a disc pommel engraved with a small lotus; keep the dominant colours maroon, golden yellow, forest green, off-white, and dark brown, and present him from multiple angles with clear views of face, hair, costume, and sword for consistent redrawing.
